source.title: Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) Moderate Baseline
source.legalReference: FedRAMP Rev.5 Baselines (based on NIST SP 800-53 Rev.5)

strategies.name: Access Control (AC)
strategies.description: Controls for managing access to cloud service offerings. Covers identity governance, privileged access management, external sharing, guest access restrictions, and information sharing agreements in the FedRAMP Moderate boundary.

ML1.summary: Establish identity and access governance foundations: disable user consent to third-party apps, configure password protection, enable security defaults, define authentication methods, implement access reviews, PIM, and baseline SharePoint external sharing controls.

ML2.summary: Enforce operational access controls: restrict application registration, enable admin consent workflows, restrict guest invitations, enforce SharePoint domain-restricted sharing, anonymous link expiry, and access request workflows.

ML3.summary: Implement advanced access governance: deploy admin consent workflow, enforce SSPR registration and token protection, and configure strict SharePoint site-level access, conditional access policies, and information barriers.

strategies.name: Audit & Accountability (AU)
strategies.description: Controls ensuring cloud service activities are auditable and traceable within the FedRAMP authorization boundary. Covers unified audit logging, compliance monitoring, and evidence preservation.

ML1.summary: Enable foundational audit capabilities: activate unified audit logging across all M365 workloads and deploy communication compliance policies for regulatory monitoring.

ML2.summary: Extend audit coverage: configure Purview Audit Premium for enhanced event capture and deploy SharePoint site-level audit and classification policies.

ML3.summary: Implement comprehensive audit: deploy eDiscovery hold policies for legal preservation and compliance investigation capabilities.

strategies.name: Contingency Planning (CP)
strategies.description: Controls for data recovery and continuity of operations within FedRAMP-authorized cloud environments. Covers retention policies, backup procedures, and recovery capabilities.

ML1.summary: Establish data recovery fundamentals: deploy retention policies for Exchange, SharePoint, and Teams content, and configure SharePoint versioning and recycle bin protections.

ML2.summary: Extend retention coverage: implement OneDrive retention policies and Teams channel message retention to protect against data loss across collaboration workloads.

ML3.summary: Implement preservation controls: deploy litigation hold policies for legal preservation, and configure retention lock to prevent policy modification.

strategies.name: Incident Response (IR)
strategies.description: Controls for detecting, analysing, containing, and recovering from security incidents in FedRAMP-authorized cloud environments. Covers endpoint detection and response, threat intelligence, and incident handling.

ML1.summary: Deploy core endpoint detection: enable Defender for Endpoint EDR, configure antivirus policies, deploy next-generation protection, and establish automated investigation capabilities.

ML2.summary: Enhance detection and response: configure vulnerability management, web content filtering, attack indicators, and network protection. Deploy DLP incident management and insider risk alerting.

ML3.summary: Implement advanced threat response: deploy device control, application guard, exploit guard, and advanced hunting. Enable insider risk investigation and communication monitoring.

strategies.name: Media Protection (MP)
strategies.description: Controls for protecting information throughout its lifecycle within FedRAMP-authorized environments. Covers data classification, sensitivity labelling, data loss prevention, and encryption for data at rest and in transit.

ML1.summary: Establish data classification foundations: deploy sensitivity labels for Confidential, Highly Confidential, and Internal use, and configure DLP policies for credit card and personal data detection.

ML2.summary: Extend data protection: deploy sensitivity label auto-labelling, encryption-enforcing labels, DLP for Exchange/Teams/SharePoint, exact data matching, endpoint DLP, and SharePoint data access governance.

ML3.summary: Implement comprehensive data controls: deploy trainable classifiers, advanced DLP tuning, information barriers, data lifecycle management, and SharePoint advanced access and versioning controls.

strategies.name: System & Communications Protection (SC)
strategies.description: Controls for protecting communications channels and securing information in transit within FedRAMP-authorized cloud environments. Covers email security, transport encryption, anti-phishing, and safe content scanning.

ML1.summary: Establish communications security: enforce TLS for Exchange transport, configure DKIM/DMARC email authentication, block auto-forwarding to external domains, and deploy baseline anti-phishing policies.

ML2.summary: Strengthen email security: enable mailbox auditing, deploy DKIM signing, configure anti-spoofing, journal rules, Safe Links URL scanning, Safe Attachments, impersonation protection, and internal phishing policies.

ML3.summary: Implement advanced communications protection: disable calendar sharing externally, configure mail flow rules for sensitive content, deploy tenant allow/block lists, and advanced Safe Links/Attachments with ZAP.

strategies.name: System & Information Integrity (SI)
strategies.description: Controls for maintaining the integrity of information and collaboration systems within FedRAMP-authorized environments. Covers Teams security, collaboration controls, and external integration restrictions.

ML1.summary: Establish collaboration integrity controls: configure Teams meeting policies to prevent anonymous join and lobby bypass, and restrict external domain access to approved organisations.

ML2.summary: Strengthen collaboration security: enforce meeting recording consent, restrict external messaging, disable unmanaged file sharing in Teams chats, and control meeting chat capabilities.

ML3.summary: Implement advanced collaboration controls: restrict external app and cloud storage access in Teams, enforce meeting watermarks, disable email-to-channel, and restrict channel creation permissions.
